text:
Emily wanted honey. She didn’t mind paying a lot for honey. She loved honey.

She went down to the bakery. There was nothing there she wanted. She wandered around a little bit. A bakery.

A guy with a white apron stepped forward from behind the oven door.

“What can I bake for you?”

“I had some honey bees last summer. That’s good stuff.”

“That honey is great.” He chuckled. “I’ve got almond.”

“Can’t beat honey. I’ll take a couple of those almond things.”

She bought two. It was nice.

She went out for a walk and walked past a quiet park. She looked at all the birds.

Then she heard someone laughing. She squatted down.

A rat!

A rat? She’d seen rats.

Not rats like that.

“Sorry.”

What a rat?

“Oh, nothing.”

“Didn’t hear?”

“Not a thing.”

“Never mind me.”

She just walked away.

The rat scampered around.<|endoftext|>
